name: Loop 10: Col de Calzan – 10 Pyrenean routes
name_original: Boucle 10 : Le col de Calzan — 10 itinéraires pyrénéens
description:
This final route that starts from the town of Pamiers offers an easier alternative compared to the difficulties of the previous loops.

There are many stops, starting with the superb town of Mirepoix, which you may have already discovered during loop 5.

The must-see on this route for me is the incredible Notre-Dame de Vals church. One of a kind, it is definitely worth a look. I'm not particularly sensitive to architecture and Christian art, but here you are in front of a very special church that leaves no one indifferent. You will go as you explore from discovery to discovery. It is located at the start of the route shortly after leaving the town of Pamiers around the 16th kilometer.

This visit done, you then follow a quiet stream to Mirepoix.

After Mirepoix, it is the Pyrenean foothills that you find. Expect more rugged terrain, but punctuated by beautiful views that will culminate at Col de Calzan. This pass presents beautiful landscapes.

After this last effort, it will be the descent towards the valley of Crieu. You will successively pass through Saint-Félix-du-Rieutord and Verniolle before returning to Pamiers, birthplace of the musician Gabriel Fauré.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Pamiers traverse a diverse landscape at the foothills of the Pyrenees in southwestern France. The region features expansive river plains, rolling hills, and challenging ascents into the Pyrenean foothills. The Ariège River flows through the area, offering relatively flat routes, while greenways, often repurposed from old railway lines, provide dedicated cycling paths. This varied topography supports a range of road cycling experiences. Best road cycling routes around Pamiers The most popular road cycling route is Loop 7: Carla-Bayle and Mas D'Azil – 10 Pyrenean routes, a 44.3 miles (71.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 27 minutes to complete. This route offers a challenging ride through the Pyrenean foothills. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Loop 10: Col de Calzan – 10 Pyrenean routes, a moderate 42.7 miles (68.7 km) path. It features significant elevation gain, characteristic of the region's mountain passes. Local road cyclists also love the Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop, a 22.1 miles (35.6 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the area around Lac de Mondély, often completed in about 2 hours 1 minute. Road cycling around Pamiers is defined by river plains, rolling hills, and Pyrenean foothills, with routes ranging from easy greenways to demanding mountain ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from casual rides to strenuous climbs. The routes in Pamiers are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. More than 6000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Pamiers's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Pamiers?
A: There are over 570 road cycling routes around Pamiers, catering to various skill levels. This includes more than 100 easy routes, over 360 moderate routes, and more than 100 difficult routes, offering a wide range of experiences from gentle rides to strenuous climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Pamiers?
A: The region around Pamiers offers a diverse topography. You'll find expansive river plains along the Ariège River, ideal for flatter rides, as well as rolling hills and the initial foothills of the majestic Pyrenees mountains for more challenging ascents. Dedicated greenways, often repurposed from old railway lines, provide smooth, traffic-free paths.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Pamiers?
A: Yes, Pamiers offers routes suitable for less experienced cyclists and families. The river plains provide relatively flat options, and greenways like the converted railway line connecting Pamiers and Mirepoix (part of the Vélosud cycle route) offer dedicated, safe paths. You can find over 100 easy routes on komoot.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Pamiers?
A: For those seeking a challenge, the Pyrenean foothills accessible from Pamiers offer ample opportunities for substantial climbs and significant elevation gains. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1206168416/loop-5-textile-country-10-pyrenean-routes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Loop 5: Textile country – 10 Pyrenean routes&lt;/a&gt;, with its nearly 1000 meters of elevation gain, provide a demanding experience. The wider Ariège region is known for its numerous 'Grand Cols' (mountain passes).

Q: What scenic attractions or landmarks can I see along road cycling routes in Pamiers?
A: Many routes allow you to integrate historical and cultural exploration. You might pass by the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2204863" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil&lt;/a&gt;, a prehistoric cave through which both a river and a road pass. Other routes can lead you to the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3597426" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Montbel&lt;/a&gt; or charming medieval towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1444029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirepoix&lt;/a&gt; and the remarkable &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3174343" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Medieval town of Camon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes around Pamiers?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Pamiers are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e937618290/grotte-du-mas-d-azil-and-lac-de-mondely-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grotte du Mas-d'Azil and Lac de Mondély Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through varied terrain and past notable attractions.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Pamiers?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 400 reviews. Road cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, from tranquil river plains to challenging Pyrenean foothills, and the opportunity to combine cycling with exploring historical sites and natural beauty.

Q: Are there specific cycle paths or greenways in the Pamiers region?
A: Yes, the region has a strong commitment to cycling infrastructure. A notable example is the converted railway line connecting Pamiers and Mirepoix, which forms a part of the popular Vélosud cycle route. These greenways provide dedicated and smooth paths, safely separated from vehicular traffic, ideal for relaxed cycling.

Q: Can I find cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the cycling routes?
A: While specific cafes are not listed for every route, the presence of charming medieval towns like Mirepoix and the general popularity of cycling in the region suggest that you will find opportunities for refreshments. The Vélosud route, for instance, passes through Pamiers and continues towards such towns, offering chances for stops.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Pamiers?
A: The region's climate generally supports cycling for much of the year. Spring and autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially in the plains, but the forested areas and higher elevations in the foothills provide cooler options. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes or multi-day tours starting from Pamiers?
A: Pamiers is well-positioned for longer rides, serving as a gateway to the Pyrenees. While specific multi-day tours aren't detailed here, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39382981" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foix – Agnès Pass loop from Varilhes&lt;/a&gt;, at over 166 km, demonstrate the potential for extensive rides. The Vélosud route also offers opportunities for longer journeys through the region.

Q: Is there parking available near popular road cycling route starting points in Pamiers?
A: While specific parking locations for every route are not detailed, Pamiers and surrounding towns are generally equipped with public parking facilities. For routes around attractions like Lake Montbel or those accessing greenways, you can typically find parking nearby. It's advisable to check local parking options for your chosen starting point.
tips: This superb so-called semi-rock church has three levels. These Romanesque frescoes and its troglodyte appearance give this 10th century religious building a special place. The place is more splendid.

The town of Mirepoix with its beautiful arcades, its colorful houses, its checkerboard streets, its beautiful wooden gargoyles and its numerous shops and cafes seems preserved from the vicissitudes of time. It’s a trip into the past that we indulge in when we walk through the city center.
This very pretty little pass has no traffic. Tranquility guaranteed!
